concept: Thirsty / Dry-Throated / In Need of Water
description: Thirsty: the dry counterpart of 'kumoli' (hungry), answered by 'phialu' (water) and 'milau' (drink).
sound_symbolism: 'ki' catches dry in the throat and 'shu' is the parched whisper — the word sounds like needing water.
grammatical_notes: Predicative: 'shia kishu nai.' (They are thirsty.) With the answer: 'no wei shia phialu loa.' (Give them water.)

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Water offered to the thirsty is hospitality's first act in every tradition; the adjective is its request form.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Wells, springs, and carried gourds — settlement followed the answers to thirst; the word walked ahead of every path to water.
